2606.03290.title: Message Tuning Outshines Graph Prompt Tuning: A Prismatic Space Perspective
2606.03290.paper_summary: Graph Foundation Models (GFMs), built upon the Pre-training and Adaptation paradigm, have emerged as a research hotspot in graph learning. For GNN-based GFMs, graph prompt tuning has become the prevailing adaptation method for downstream tasks. Although recent methods explain why graph prompt tuning works, how to rigorously measure its adaptation capacity remains an open problem. Addressing this problem is critical for understanding the capability limits of graph prompt tuning and for developing more powerful adaptation methods. In this paper, we propose Prismatic Space Theory (PS-Theory), a novel mathematical framework to quantify the capacity of adaptation methods, while focusing on establishing the upper bound for the adaptation capacity of graph prompt tuning. Building upon the proposed PS-Theory, we further introduce Message Tuning for GFMs (MTG), a lightweight approach that injects a small set of learnable message prototypes into each layer of the GNN backbone to adaptively guide message fusion without updating pre-trained weights. Through our PS-Theory, we prove that the adaptation capacity of MTG can exceed the theoretical upper bound of graph prompt tuning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MTG consistently outperforms graph prompt baselines across diverse benchmark datasets, providing strong empirical support for our theoretical findings.
2606.03290.paper_summary_zh: 圖形基礎模型（GFMs）建立在預訓練和適應的範式上，已成為圖形學習中的研究熱點。對於基於GNN的GFMs，圖形提示調整已成為下游任務的主要適應方法。儘管最近的方法解釋了為什麼圖形提示調整有效，但如何嚴格測量其適應能力仍然是一個未解決的問題。解決這個問題對於理解圖形提示調整的能力極限以及開發更強大的適應方法至關重要。在本文中，我們提出了棱鏡空間理論（PS-Theory），這是一個新穎的數學框架，用於量化適應方法的能力，同時專注於建立圖形提示調整的適應能力的上限。在所提出的PS-Theory的基礎上，我們進一步介紹了GFMs的消息調整（MTG），這是一種輕量級的方法，將一小組可學習的消息原型注入到GNN主幹的每一層，以自適應地指導消息融合，而無需更新預訓練的權重。通過我們的PS-Theory，我們證明了MTG的適應能力可以超過圖形提示調整的理論上限。大量實驗表明，MTG在各種基準數據集上始終優於圖形提示基線，為我們的理論發現提供了強有力的實證支持。
2606.03290.author: Yancheng Chen et.al.
2606.03290.authors: Yancheng Chen, Dun Ma, Shuai Zhang, Yang Liu, Xixun Lin, Xiangyu Zhao, Wenguo Yang, Wei Chen, Chuan Zhou
